description_long: This sculpture is a rough-hewn stone artwork depicting a group of five human figures standing closely together on a rectangular base. Four of the figures are adult-sized, standing upright with their arms crossed or at their sides, while a smaller child figure stands at the front right. The figures are stylized with simplified, blocky forms and minimal facial features, giving the piece a solemn, monolithic appearance. The surface is heavily textured, with a coarse, weathered stone finish that suggests age and durability. The figures are arranged in a tight cluster, emphasizing unity and collective presence. The entire sculpture is carved from a single block of stone, with no visible separations or joints.
description: A rough-textured stone sculpture of five stylized human figures standing closely together on a rectangular base. The group includes four adults and one child, all rendered in a simplified, blocky style.
description_view_0: Front view showing four adult figures standing side by side, arms crossed or at their sides, with detailed faces and torsos visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the figures and their close arrangement, with arms and bodies overlapping.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the figures and the smaller child figure standing at the front right, with less facial detail visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the figures' profiles and the compact grouping, highlighting the sculpture's thickness.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
